subject: Mathematics
title: Toy Joy

sections.heading: Toy Joy
sections.content:
Use old boxes (cubes and cuboids) and
bottles (cylinders) to do craft work. Draw
faces on them.
Some children are making animal and bird faces on old
boxes, cans and paper.
What did you draw on their faces?
Jaya is building a rocket with different shapes.
What shapes has she used?
How many of each?
Cube: _____________ Cuboid: _____________
Cone: _____________ Cylinder: _____________
Which shape is between the red cuboid and yellow cuboid?
Which shape is on the top of the orange cylinder?
Which shape is under the pink cone?
I made a bird face
on the cone. Look at
its pointed beak!
Toy JoyToy Joy
Let us Do
Look at my bottle
(cylinder). I made a
puppy face on it. It
has wide ears.
I made a pig face
on the box (cube). It has
narrow eyes.
I made a cat
face on the cuboid. It has
long whiskers.
face 
corner
edge
Reprint 2026-27
10Maths M ela | Class 3
Collect objects to make a house. Name the shapes, and talk
about their faces and edges. Which are straight and which are
curved? Also, describe how the shapes have been arranged.
What part of the shapes can you see from a distance?
____________ ____________ ____________ ____________
____________ ____________ ____________ ____________
Jaya made some houses using different shapes.
Try to build such houses, towers, rockets, etc., using
different shapes available around you.
Here are some more toys from the toy shop. They are
made up of different shapes.
What shapes are used in these toys?
Devika went to a shop and bought a toy engine.
Here is Devika’s toy engine. It has many
parts. Count and fill.
________ red cylinder(s) ________ yellow cone(s)
________ grey cuboid(s) ________ blue cube(s)
Let us Discuss
Note that a cube is a special type of cuboid.
Reprint 2026-27
11Toy J oy
Construct and Describe
Ask students to sit in groups of four or
five. In each group, one student selects any
three shapes and puts them together. The
student, then describes the sequence of
construction and the other students have to
build the same without seeing the original
one. Let children take turns and play the
game in the group.
Example
The cylinder is of the cuboid. The cone is on
the cylinder.
Let us Play
Let us Do
1. Find the shapes in the classroom that look like the following
shapes. And fill in the table with their names.
(a) Name the shape that you find the most. ____________
(b) Name the shape that you find the least. ____________
(c) Name the objects that are made up of more
than one shape. ____________
Cylinder Sphere Cube Cuboid Cone
Reprint 2026-27
• Circle the cubes.
• Put a tick ü against the
shapes that are cones.
• Put a cross  against the
shapes that are cylinders.
• Draw a box around the
cuboids.
Teacher’s Note: Let students look for different shapes in
the classroom. Ask them to share more examples of each
shape. Talk about opposite faces in cubes, cuboids and
cylinders. They can turn the shapes and observe them in
different orientations.
2. Look at these shapes and follow the instructions
below.
3. Name the shapes:
• with no edges. ______________
• with only flat faces. ______________
• with only curved faces. ______________
• with both straight and
curved edges. ______________
• with both flat and curved faces. ______________
Reprint 2026-27
4. Which shapes can you build?
Look what I made with clay
and sticks! You can also try to
make such shapes.
5. Try to make these shapes using cubes.
Let us Play
There is a game along the border of these pages (12, 13). Roll
the die and move the counter to the next picture associated
with the number on the die (if possible without crossing the
‘Finish’ square) and play the game with your friends. The one
who reaches the finish mark first wins the game.
– Move 2 steps forward if possible
– Cylinder – Sphere – Cone – Cube
– Cuboid
Reprint 2026-27
14Maths M ela | Class 3
8. In what order is this model built?
7. Look at a die. The faces have 1 to 6 dots.
What number is on:
• the face opposite number 1? _____________
• the face opposite number 2? _____________
• the face opposite number 3? _____________
• What pattern do you notice? _____________
6. In what ways are these shapes the same? In what
ways are they different?
Reprint 2026-27
15Toy J oy
9. In how many different ways can you join 3 cubes? Try and see.
What shape do
you get if you join a
cube and a cuboid?
How many
cubes are used to
make
this shape?
10. Name the shapes used in these models. Describe how
the shapes are arranged.
11. Use six dice to make the following shapes:
(a) (b) (c) (d)
(a) a cuboid (b) a tower (c) any other shape of your choice
What possible
shapes do you
get if you
join 3 cubes?
Reprint 2026-27

learningObjectives: Understand the key concepts of Toy Joy
Identify and explain the main ideas covered in this chapter
Apply knowledge from this chapter to real-world situations
Recall important terms and definitions from Toy Joy
